title: Analytics - Firmware Update Started
scenario: Firebase analytics "firmware_update_started" event does not display device-specific parameters (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Target Language ID) after DFU starts on Poly Bluetooth device
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Connect to a Poly Bluetooth audio device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200, Poly Savi 8200, etc.).
3. Navigate to Poly Studio Mobile → [Device Name] → Device Settings.
4. Initiate a DFU (firmware update) process from the Device Settings screen.
5. Wait for the firmware update to start (the "firmware_update_started" event should be logged to Firebase Analytics).
6. Open the Firebase Analytics console and navigate to the "firmware_update_started" event page.
7. Verify that the following default elements are displayed: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment.
8. Verify that the following device-specific elements are also displayed: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Target Language ID.
9. Wait a few hours after the DFU was initiated.
10. Return to the Firebase Analytics console and check the event count for "firmware_update_started".
11. Verify that the event count has incremented by at least 1 (reflecting the DFU started in step 4).
expected: The Firebase Analytics "firmware_update_started" event page displays all default elements (graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment) and device-specific elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Target Language ID). After a few hours, the event count for "firmware_update_started" increments by at least 1.
